Patrick Mullins interested prehistoric frontiers, coastal-highland interaction, warfare, fortifications Andes.
During Late Intermediate Period (1000-1470 AD), Chimú Empire heavily fortified hinterland that spread from their capital Chan Chan, Peru's Pacific coast, perhaps upper tributaries Moche River.
Patrick's research aims understanding extent nature fortified Chimú hinterland interactions that created shared frontier between coastal Chimú, middle/upper valley, highland settlements.
spent part summer 2013 surveying Neolithic Iron Ages sites Serbia.
